name: MCP Server Apple Events
what_it_does: MCP server enabling AI agents to send Apple Events — the macOS inter-application communication protocol used by AppleScript. Allows agents to automate macOS applications (Finder, Mail, Calendar, Safari, etc.) by sending Apple Events programmatically, enabling system-level macOS automation from AI agents.
use_cases: Automating macOS applications (Finder, Mail, Safari) from macOS automation agents
Controlling Apple apps via AppleScript-compatible Apple Events from AI assistants
Building macOS workflow automation that interacts with scriptable applications
Integrating macOS system capabilities into AI-driven productivity workflows
not_for: Non-macOS platforms (Windows, Linux) — Apple Events is macOS-only
Applications that don't support Apple Events/AppleScript
Sandboxed App Store apps with restricted automation permissions
best_when: A macOS power user or developer wants AI agents to automate Mac applications using Apple Events — leveraging macOS's built-in inter-application automation for system-level workflows.
avoid_when: You're not on macOS, or your target applications don't support Apple Events. Also not suitable for automating sandboxed Mac App Store apps with restricted entitlements.

auth.notes: No authentication — macOS Automation permission required. System will prompt for Automation permission on first use.

performance.rate_limits: Local — bounded by macOS application response times

known_agent_gotchas: PHYSICAL EFFECTS: Controls real macOS applications — test thoroughly before autonomous agent use
macOS Automation permission required — system will prompt; grant carefully per-app
Apple Events syntax is app-specific — agents need knowledge of target app's Apple Events dictionary
macOS updates can change Apple Events behavior — test against your macOS version
agent_readiness.security_notes: macOS Automation permission required. Controls real Mac applications including Mail, Calendar, and Finder — potential data exfiltration risk if misused. Grant minimal automation permissions. macOS local only.

security_score_components.security_notes: macOS app control including Mail and Files. Automation permission required per-app. Local only. Restrict to specific apps needed.
